title: How to use paired-end information for graph decomposition
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"rql7","text":"In order to decompose a complecated metagenomic de Bruijn graph into simpler subgraphs, MetaVelvet searches the following X-structure, i.e., chimeric nodes have two incoming edges and two outgoing edges.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eotpd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"davda","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"6on5b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cimqf","text":"Then, MetaVelvet split the X-structures when the following conditions are satisfied: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2st7p","text":"Primary-IN and Primary-OUT nodes are classified into the same peak.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e2f20","text":"Secondary-IN and Secondary-OUT nodes are classified into the same peak.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"clsos","text":"Chimeric node has a coverage value mostly equal (within 50% difference by default) to the average between the sum of coverage values of the two origin nodes of incoming edges (Primary-IN and Secondary-IN), and the sum of the two destination nodes of outgoing edges (Primary-OUT and Secondary-OUT).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"mt2c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cv80n","text":"In order to accurately avoid chimeric contigs/scaffolds caused by subgraph decomposition, we added the following two conditions (>= 1.2.01):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6vdfu","text":"The number of consistent paired-end connections is equal to or larger than a certain value. This cutoff value can be specified by the -valid_connections option (default: 1).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":14,"length":33},{"style":"bold","offset":134,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bj0qv","text":"The number of inconsistent paired-end connections is equal to or smaller than a certain value. This cutoff value can be specified by the -noise_connections option (default: 0).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":14,"length":35},{"style":"bold","offset":137,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ebabi","text":"For lower compatibility, users can turn off the paired-end conditions by the -use_connections option.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":77,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fvecn","text":"","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cilbe","text":"Here, we denote paired-end connections between Primary-IN and Primary-OUT or between Secondary-IN and Secondary-OUT as consistent paired-end connections. We also denote paired-end connections between Primary-IN and Secondary-OUT or between Secondary-IN and Primary-OUT as inconsistent paired-end connections. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":119,"length":33},{"style":"italic","offset":272,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"adgu3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4m6e6","text":"Based upon our accuracy evaluation, -valid_connections 1 -noise_connections 0 is an appropriate setting when very similar species co-exist in an environment (MetaVelvet can efficiently avoid misassemblies). Otherwise, -valid_connections 0 -noise_connections 0 is a more appropriate setting (MetaVelvet can achieve a greater contig/scaffold N50 while avoiding misassemblies).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":36,"length":41},{"style":"bold","offset":218,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4uhck","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cpo3n","text":"Frequently Asked Questions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3l79i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3b4pl","text":"Q: meta-velveth is not generated in the new version (>= 1.1.01). Is it problem? 
A: This is not problem. The usage of MetaVelvet is changed when the version 1.1.01 is released, and the new version does not include meta-velveth. Instead, please use velveth, velvetg, and meta-velvetg.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ae97","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2e7u6","text":"Q: When only one coverage peak is detected (or manually input), is there any difference between MetaVelvet and Velvet algorithms? 
A: There is no substantial difference. In such cases, meta-velvetg moves to "single-peak mode" and graph splitting functions in meta-velvetg is not called. Instead of graph splitting functions, standard velvet functions are called in such cases.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b9jto","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5pe97","text":"Q: What's the difference in working procedures between velvetg, meta-velvetg (<= 0.3.1), and meta-velvetg(>= 1.1.01)? 
A: The following is the working procedure of velvetg, meta-velvetg (<= 0.3.1), and meta-velvetg (>= 1.1.01): 

    velvetg & meta-velvetg(<=0.3.1) : 
        Load Sequences & Roadmaps file 
        -> Generate PreGraph file 
        -> Generate Graph or Graph2 file 
        -> Generate contigs.fa and LastGraph 

    meta-velvetg (>= 1.1.01): 
        Load Sequences & Roadmaps & Graph2 file 
        -> Generate meta-velvetg.contigs.fa and meta-velvetg.LastGraph ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdp0m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4l6qa","text":"Q: Is version compatibility between Velvet and MetaVelvet fully tested? 
A: Version compatibility between Velvet-1.0.06 and MetaVelvet-1.1.01 is fully tested.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4bi09","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ae0i9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cs5mr","text":"Trouble:When drawingk-mer coverage histogram (as in the"Advanced topics 1"section), the following warning messages is appeared:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":55,"length":19}],"data":[]},{"key":"4pn7r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"afa5j","text":"> weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov,data$lgth,breaks=seq(0,200,1)) 
Warning messages: 
1: In min(x, na.rm = na.rm) : 
no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf 
2: In max(x, na.rm = na.rm) : 
no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf 
3: In weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov, data$lgth, breaks = seq(0, 200, : 
Areas will not relate to frequencies ","type":"blockquote","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10kp7","text":"Solution:This warning (error) is caused by "Inf" values in the Graph2 node stats. Accordingly, by removing "Inf" values from the Graph2 stats, the error is resolved:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d6r9o","text":"
$ head -n 1 meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt \ 
        > meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt 
$ perl -ne '{print $_ unless /Inf/;}' \ 
        meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt \ 
        >> meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt 
$ R 
> library(plotrix) 
> data = read.table("meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt", header=TRUE) 
> weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov,data$lgth,breaks=seq(0,200,1)) ","type":"blockquote","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"AF3DDCF204B44D77B26E41FB3F824579","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"fig7.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/bngc2w6.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bngc2w6.png","width":null}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5075668EBBDD46509765836A7E942759","url":"http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/MV.html#advanced-topic-1"}}}}
creator: Bonnie L Hurwitz
